rulings.Case Title: GEORGE BALLARD COMPANY, A CORPORATION VS. PAMELA A. ERB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT PAMELA ERB MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Motion to set aside default and default judgment granted pursuant to CCP section 473. Defendant to pay $1,018 as reasonable attorney fees and costs. The liens are vacated. Defendant to file answer within five days. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Cross-Deft Eren Hussein'S Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike The First And Second Causes Of Action Of The Cross-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFFS GARY GROTE, JANICE GROTE, and, EREN HUSSEIN Motion to Strike the First and Second Causes of Action of The Cross-Complaint. Anti-Slapp motion denied. Cross-defendant fails to make a prima facie showing of protected activity. This home owners association not a public forum. The website is not a public forum in that posted documents did not encourage a public exchange or debate. No showing that the statements published were public interest statements. All objections overruled. Request to strike or seal documents denied without prejudice. All requests for sanctions and attorney fees denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA COUNTY SUPERINTENDENTS EDUCATIONAL VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION ON THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM (CALPERS), KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, and, SHAREN SCOTT DEMURRER. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action for declaratory relief. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for violation of due process and 42 U.S.C. 1983. These claims were brought in the federal court action against the same individual defendants and dismissed with prejudice in March 2009. The primary rights doctrine bars plaintiffs' claims in the current suit. No showing that public policy and injustice exceptions to res judicata would apply. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WAI MAN CHAN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 4, 2009, line 9, PETITIONER WAI CHAN Notice Of Hearing for Writ of Mandate. Petition for Writ denied without prejudice. Petitioner to re-calendar this matter after serving the real party in interest. In other words, petitioner needs to serve her former employer with this petition. =(302/CWW)
